name: Taiwan (TSMC)
summary: Day 76. **C4 TAIEX 41,751.75 (+0.9% 1D, +15.0% 1M, +23.6% 3M) — AI SUPER-CYCLE CONTINUES**. TSMC concentration dominant; same AI super-cycle as KOSPI (+1.2%/+36.7% 1M); SMH $572.46 (+2.0% 1D, +26.7% 1M, +40.5% 3M); NASDAQ Composite 26,402.34 (+1.2% 1D, +11.7% 1M). **HELIUM 2-WEEK-CLOCK at Day 73 since Ras Laffan halt March 2; helium-clock-expiry window MAY 16-23** — Taiwan is one of three indices most exposed to helium-shortage-forced-fab-shutdown along with KOSPI/SMH. Per markets dossier H reversibility map: KOSPI/Taiwan are most asymmetric DOWN trades if forced fab shutdown triggers (-15-25% to -20-30% snap-back). Per markets dossier C4: AI super-cycle is dominant global equity narrative — NOT a war-relief trade and would NOT reverse on war-resolution; would collapse violently on helium-shortage-forced-fab-shutdown. Trump-Xi summit Day 1 'partners not rivals' opening = MSCI EM +2.1% with Taiwan participating. PRIOR (May 7): **TAIEX 41,933.78 (+1.9% 1D, +26.2% 1M, +29.4% 3M) — TSMC CONTINUED DOMINANCE AMID APPLE-SAMSUNG-INTEL CHIP-PRODUCTION TALKS.** Apple's potential diversification beyond TSMC (Apple-Samsung-Intel talks) is a forward demand-channel risk, but TSMC's structural position remains dominant in node leadership. SMH +5.2% to $549.76 (+37.5% 1M) is TSMC ecosystem confirmation. Helium cascade falsification stays intact at tier-1 chipmaker level. Chip complex decoupled from crisis (~10% crisis attribution); ~90% AI capex driven. Patel/SemiAnalysis bifurcation thesis sharpens with today's peace-dividend session adding tailwind on top. Today's TAIEX continuation confirms AI-cycle decoupling thesis remains intact post-MOU framework — peace-dividend pricing + AI cycle compounding.

active_triggers.condition: TSMC announces production reduction or allocation changes due to helium/gas supply
